{"query":"Berkshire Hathaway 13F 2026 Delta Air Lines Macy's new positions shares value Greg Abel portfolio changes","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1541,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/berkshire-hathaway-just-made-fresh-171520177.html","title":"The New Berkshire Hathaway Just Made a Fresh Bet on a Department Store Everyone Wrote Off","content":"Greg Abel's first Berkshire move bought 3 million Macy's shares for ~$55M, targeting a cash-generating retailer Wall Street had written off.
Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) disclosed a brand-new 3.04 million-share stake in Macy's (NYSE:M) in its Q1 2026 13F filing, revealed on May 15, 2026, the first quarterly portfolio reshuffle under Greg Abel's tenure as CEO."},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/18/berkshire-portfolio-revamp-delta-airlines-macys-unitedhealth.html","title":"Berkshire Hathaway portfolio changes: How the new stocks are trading","content":"The Omaha-based conglomerate disclosed its quarter-end equity holdings in a regulatory filing Friday, offering one of the first looks at the portfolio under new CEO Greg Abel, who took over at the start of the year from legendary investor Warren Buffett.
One of the biggest moves from the latest filing was a sizeable new stake in Delta Air Lines.
Delta shares jumped more than 3% at one point Monday.
The department store shares climbed more than 1% Monday after the filing revealed Berkshire took a new stake valued at roughly $55 million at the end of the first quarter.
Meanwhile, the Omaha-based conglomerate significantly increased its relatively new position in Alphabet, making the Google parent its seventh-largest holding.
Other stocks Berkshire sold included UnitedHealth Group, Aon, Pool Corporation, Domino's Pizza and Charter Communications."},{"url":"https://www.investopedia.com/berkshire-hathaway-13f-q1-2026-stocks-greg-abel-warren-buffett-11976563","title":"Berkshire Bought New Stocks, Shed Some Big Names in First Quarter Without Buffett as CEO","content":"The company also cut its stakes in Amazon, Mastercard, UnitedHealth, Visa and several others.
The conglomerate added new stakes in Delta Air Lines (DAL) and Macy's (M) during Greg Abel's first quarter as CEO, a regulatory filing Friday showed.
Berkshire's (BRK.A, BRK.B) new stake in Delta totaled 39.8 million shares at the end of the first quarter, while it held roughly 3 million shares of Macy's, making both far smaller stakes than Berkshire's largest holdings.
The choices to exit those stocks could potentially point to changes Abel's made to offload the picks of Todd Combs, who left Berkshire for JPMorgan at the end of 2025."},{"url":"https://www.benzinga.com/trading-ideas/long-ideas/26/05/52618148/buffett-steps-aside-abels-first-berkshire-13f-buys-delta-air-lines-exits-visa-and-mastercard","title":"Buffett Steps Aside: Abel's First Berkshire 13F Buys Delta Air Lines, Exits Visa And Mastercard - Delta A - Benzinga","content":"Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL): Add 39,809,456 shares
The first-quarter 13F showed changes in the existing stocks held by Berkshire Hathaway.
Chevron Corporation (NYSE:CVX): -35%
Bank of America: $25.0 billion"},{"url":"https://investsnips.com/warren-buffett-stock-portfolio/","title":"Warren Buffett Stock Portfolio 2026: Full Berkshire Hathaway Holdings, Recent Buys & Sells | InvestSnips","content":"Berkshire Hathaway's portfolio positions change each quarter and are disclosed with a 45-day delay via SEC Form 13F.
Total portfolio value ~$274.2B across 42 disclosed positions.
Still ~22.6% of disclosed portfolio — Berkshire's largest single holding.
What changes under Abel: Investment decision-making for large capital deployments may now involve the input of investment managers Todd Combs and Ted Weschler more explicitly, though Buffett retains influence as chairman.
Berkshire's 13F is filed 45 days after each quarter ends: February 14 (Q4), May 15 (Q1), August 14 (Q2), and November 14 (Q3).
Berkshire's AXP position has been held for 30+ years through multiple cycles because of conviction in American Express's premium customer loyalty model — not because AXP happened to be in Buffett's 13F.
BRK/B (Class B) shares trade at approximately 1/1,500 the price of BRK/A (roughly $460–$500) and carry 1/10,000 vote — but they represent the same 1/1,500 of BRK/A's economic interest."}]}
